objective: Implement fork-only reliability and quality optimization for RunPod vLLM to support Qwen3.5-27B at 128k context with minimal quality degradation and deterministic container behavior.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible changes in this fork only to .runpod/hub.json, src/engine_args.py, Dockerfile, README.md, and aiwf.request.json: make Qwen presets explicitly 128k quality-first, keep optional numeric overrides explicitly unset, preserve strict invalid-zero override handling with added Qwen 128k guidance warnings, make Docker nightly build path deterministic and fail-fast when pinned nightly install fails, and document 128k quality mode guidance.
acceptance_criteria: .runpod/hub.json Qwen presets set MAX_MODEL_LEN=131072, SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP=131072, ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASH_ATTN, KV_CACHE_DTYPE=auto, ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=true, LANGUAGE_MODEL_ONLY=true, and conservative MAX_CONCURRENCY/MAX_NUM_SEQS values (1/1 safe, 2/2 balanced, 4/4 throughput).
.runpod/hub.json keeps NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE, MAX_CPU_LORAS, and MAX_PARALLEL_LOADING_WORKERS as explicit empty strings and descriptions clearly state empty means unset and 0 is forbidden.
src/engine_args.py does not silently hide Qwen 128k degradation: it keeps strict zero-override handling and emits non-fatal quality guidance when effective MAX_MODEL_LEN is below 131072 for Qwen3.5.
Dockerfile defaults VLLM_NIGHTLY to false and removes floating nightly fallback so pinned nightly install failures fail the build.
README.md includes a short 128k quality mode section and notes KV fp8 as optional capacity mode with potential quality impact.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Rollback path is an immediate fork-local revert of this change set (git revert of the runtime optimization commit) restoring previous .runpod/hub.json presets, prior src/engine_args.py guidance behavior, previous Dockerfile nightly default/fallback logic, and README/aiwf.request.json text. If runtime instability appears after deploy, roll back endpoint image tag to the last known-good fork release while revert build completes.
deploy_plan: Deploy in staged fork-only rollout (1->2->4 workers) using Qwen safe preset first. Validate cold start, model load, and first-token latency at 128k settings, then run representative long-context prompts before shifting traffic to balanced and throughput presets.
threat_model: Primary risks are quality regression from aggressive KV cache settings, hidden context cap regressions, and nondeterministic build outcomes from floating nightly installs. Mitigations: quality-first preset defaults (KV auto), explicit 128k caps, Qwen max-length guidance warnings, deterministic stable-default Docker path, and fail-fast pinned nightly install behavior.
